context: Tests whether the agent recognises a Java-style value class (manual equals/hashCode/toString, Java-bean accessors, var properties) and refactors it to the idiomatic Kotlin shape — data class, val properties, no manual override of standard object methods, no bean accessors. Field order must be preserved so positional construction at call sites still compiles. Exercises K-1 (prefer-val), K-3 (use-data-class), and the pojoify-to-dataclass skill's workflow.

checklist.name: Declared as data class
checklist.description: The output declares `data class Subscription(...)` with a primary constructor — not a regular `class Subscription { ... }`

checklist.name: Properties use val
checklist.description: Every property in the primary constructor uses `val` (not `var`). The output has no `var` declarations on the Subscription type

checklist.name: No manual equals/hashCode/toString
checklist.description: The output contains no `override fun equals(...)`, `override fun hashCode()`, or `override fun toString()` — the compiler generates them for data classes

checklist.name: No Java-bean accessors
checklist.description: The output contains no `fun getX(...)`, `fun setX(...)`, or `fun isX(...)` method declarations. Property access uses Kotlin's auto-generated accessors instead

checklist.name: Field order preserved
checklist.description: The primary constructor parameters appear in this exact order: subscriptionId, planCode, status, billingPeriodMonths, autoRenew, createdAtEpochMillis, renewsAtEpochMillis. This preserves componentN() destructuring for any callers that relied on it

checklist.name: All seven fields retained
checklist.description: All seven fields from the original class are present in the new data class — none accidentally dropped during refactor
